content:
Welcome to the Mack Lab The Mack Lab is interested in how we learn. We study how attention (selecting relevant information from our experiences) interacts with memory (what we know and remember) during learning. Making sense of this interaction informs not only how learning succeeds, but also why it goes awry and how to put it back on track. Recent News New paper on functional footprints published in PNAS! 27 February 2026 Congratulations, Dr. Gumus! 20 August 2025 Congratulations, Dr. Perovic! 21 July 2025 Our computational cognitive neuroscience lab, led by Associate Professor Michael Mack, is in the Department of Psychology at the University of Toronto. We explore the learning brain with a combination of behavioural experiments, computational modelling, and functional neuroimaging. Learn more about our research and publications, who we are, and how to get involved!
Research Members Participate

content: Attention is a filtering mechanism that helps us focus on what matters. We investigate how attentional biases are formed and how they influence learning.
summary: Attention is a filtering mechanism that helps us focus on what matters. We investigate how attentional biases are formed and how they influence learning.
title: Attentional Processes

content: How do we learn to group items into categories? Our lab explores the underlying cognitive and neural processes involved in category acquisition.
summary: How do we learn to group items into categories? Our lab explores the underlying cognitive and neural processes involved in category acquisition.
title: Category Learning

content: Using fMRI and other neuroimaging techniques, we map the neural correlates of learning and memory.
summary: Using fMRI and other neuroimaging techniques, we map the neural correlates of learning and memory.
title: Neural Mechanisms

content:
Our latest research on functional footprints has been published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS).
Check it out to learn more about how attention and memory interact during learning!

content:

We are so proud of your hard work and successful defense. Well done!

content:
We are always looking for motivated undergraduate and graduate students, as well as postdoctoral fellows, who are interested in the intersection of attention and memory.
If you are interested in joining the Mack Lab, please send an email to Dr. Michael Mack (michael.mack@utoronto.ca) with your CV and a brief statement of your research interests.

title: Join the Lab

content:
Interested in participating in our studies? We are frequently looking for participants for our behavioral and neuroimaging experiments!
How to sign up # Please check out our current studies on the University of Toronto Psychology Participation Pool or contact us directly at [EMAIL_ADDRESS] for more information.
